steps.title: Track the author's stance
steps.content: The author grants that a late date for Beowulf is not inconceivable. The key objection is that supporters have not justified privileging contextual fit over linguistic evidence for an earlier composition.

steps.title: Match the main idea
steps.content: Choice A vaguely says evidence was called into question. Choice B shifts to controversy over social conditions. Choice C invents equal historical fit. Choice D captures both the plausibility of the late-date claim and the unresolved earlier-date evidence. The answer is D.
